name: pg_serializable_write_skew
description: Classic write-skew anomaly under PostgreSQL Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI). Two concurrent transactions each read both x and y, then conditionally update one. Under true serializability SSI detects the dangerous structure and aborts one transaction.
references: Cahill, Röhm, Fekete. Serializable Isolation for Snapshot Databases. TODS 2009
Fekete et al. Making Snapshot Isolation Serializable. TODS 2005
setup_sql: CREATE TABLE doctors (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL, on_call BOOLEAN NOT NULL)
schedule: T1: SELECT * FROM doctors WHERE on_call = true  -- sees Alice and Bob on-call
T2: SELECT * FROM doctors WHERE on_call = true  -- sees Alice and Bob on-call
T1: UPDATE doctors SET on_call = false WHERE id = 1
T2: UPDATE doctors SET on_call = false WHERE id = 2
T2: COMMIT  -- SSI detects dangerous structure, ABORTS with serialization failure (40001)

expected_outcome.ssi_behavior: PostgreSQL SSI detects rw-dependency cycle T1→T2→T1 and aborts T2
expected_outcome.without_ssi: Both commits succeed, leaving zero doctors on-call (constraint violation)

verification_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM doctors WHERE on_call = true;  -- Should be 1 (Bob still on-call)
